title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss, traded from the Raiders for a fourth-round pick, set an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in 2007.
Wes Welker, acquired in 2007 from the Dolphins, became a key slot receiver alongside Moss despite not winning a Super Bowl.
Josh Gordon joined the Patriots in 2018, demonstrating potential but was hindered by off-field issues and suspensions.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora ejected in 8th inning for arguing a strike three call against Trevor Story in loss to Reds.
Cora criticized umpire C.B. Bucknor's decision not to consult first base on check swing call, but acknowledged umpires' challenging role.
Boston exhausted challenges early; Roman Anthony admitted fault for aggressive challenge ending team's ability to contest calls.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story was upset with umpire CB Bucknor's strike three call, leading to manager Alex Cora's ejection.
Story argued Bucknor didn't confirm his check swing with the first base umpire during a critical moment in the game.
Despite Story's strikeout, the Red Sox tied the game but ultimately lost 6-5 to remain at a 1-1 season record.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha scored 11 goals in 16 games for the Bruins since returning from the Olympic break.
An injury kept Zacha out of the Olympics, motivating him to improve his performance for Boston.
Zacha, traded from New Jersey, surpassed his career-high with 26 goals this season for the Bruins.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray allowed four runs in four innings during his Red Sox debut against his former team, the Cincinnati Reds.
Manager Alex Cora noted Gray's performance improved after a tough first inning, while crediting the bullpen's strong outings.
The Red Sox bullpen excelled after Gray's exit, though Boston ultimately lost 6-5 in an 11-inning matchup.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 150,000 people attended the 'No Kings' protest at Boston Common, part of a nationwide demonstration against authoritarianism.
The rally featured speeches by Gov. Maura Healey, Sen. Elizabeth Warren, and performances by Dropkick Murphys, among others.
Organizers set up action tables for attendees to join advocacy groups, emphasizing democracy and resistance against political threats.

title: Review & setlist: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
author: Christopher Hurley
bullets: GWAR performed a 17-song set at House of Blues, including a cover of Chappell Roan's 'Pink Pony Club'.
The concert featured heavy metal music, fake blood, and theatrical acts with a mythical alien mastodon theme.
The show included satire targeting political figures and concluded with a tribute to late member Dave Brockie.

title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Celtics list four players as questionable, including Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum, for Sunday's game against the Hornets.
Jaylen Brown is day-to-day with left Achilles tendinitis; Nikola Vucevic remains out with a right ring finger fracture.
Jayson Tatum plays highest minutes and scores since return, struggles with shot consistency and handling despite recent win.

title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Thousands attended the 'No Kings' protest at Boston Common, part of over 160 rallies across Massachusetts.
The event featured speeches from political figures like Gov. Maura Healey and performances by Dropkick Murphys.
Protesters, including veterans and church groups, voiced concerns over democracy's future and the country's direction.

title: Photo scroll: Scenes from Boston’s ‘No Kings’ protest on March 28
author: Emily Turner
bullets: Thousands gathered at Boston Common for the 'No Kings' protest on March 28, part of over 160 statewide events.
Governor Maura Healey and Senators Edward Markey and Elizabeth Warren addressed the Boston Common crowd at the protest.
The 'No Kings' rally featured attendees walking through the Public Garden and speeches covering a range of topics.
